meaning: n.(日本)阿伊努人,阿伊努语
examples: The Ainu settlers in Hokkaido are the last remnant of a prehistoric race.
Today, only vestigialcommunities of Ainu survive.
Shiretoko's original inhabitants, the Ainu, had referred to the peninsula as the end of the world.
The Ainu are an indigenous people who today live mostly in Hokkaid? in northern Japan.
Believing that deities can be found in their surroundings, the Ainu frequently use dance to worship and give thanks for nature.
